cves.summary: Protestware: maintainer intentionally added code to overwrite files with a peace symbol emoji on systems with Russian/Belarusian IP addresses. Affected vue-cli and hundreds of downstream packages.

aiExplanation.summary: This repository contains confirmed protestware (CVE-2022-23812) where the maintainer deliberately added geolocation-based file destruction targeting Russian and Belarusian IP addresses. This affected vue-cli and millions of downstream users.

items.title: node-ipc@9.2.2 — Intentional Protestware
items.why_dangerous: The package maintainer deliberately published code that checks the user's IP geolocation and overwrites files with a peace symbol emoji if the IP resolves to Russia or Belarus. This is intentional sabotage by a trusted maintainer — the worst kind of supply chain attack.
items.exact_fix: Pin node-ipc to >=10.1.1 in package.json. Run npm ci. Add a pre-install script to audit for known bad package versions.
items.real_example: This is the real node-ipc incident from March 2022. It directly affected vue-cli which has 30+ million weekly downloads. Many enterprise CI pipelines were disrupted.
